_meta.description: D6 fixtures for hermes / a2ui-fixed-schema (A2UI Fixed Schema flight card). The demo routes to /api/copilotkit-a2ui-fixed-schema, a dedicated hermes runtime whose page registers the flight catalog via `<CopilotKit a2ui={{ catalog }}>` (auto-enabling A2UI with injectA2UITool defaulting on). Hermes has no A2UI backend and a generic aimock agent cannot emit an agent-side tool RESULT the middleware would see, so — unlike langgraph's backend display_flight tool that emits an a2ui_operations container — the agent EMITS the middleware-injected render_a2ui directly with the FIXED flight component schema as `components` + the flight `data` model. The schema's `{ path: "/origin" }` bindings resolve against that data, preserving the fixed-schema semantics. The middleware forwards the surface; the frontend flight catalog (Card/Title/Airport/Arrow/AirlineBadge/PriceTag/Button) renders it with the a2ui-fixed-card testid. Tool-emit leg gates on hasToolResult:false; narration on toolCallId.

fixtures._comment: a2ui-fixed-schema pill — 'Find me a flight from SFO to JFK on United for $289.' (D5 gen-ui-a2ui-fixed probe prompt). Agent emits the middleware-injected render_a2ui with the fixed flight schema + data. Gated on hasToolResult:false so it fires on the tool-call turn.

match.userMessage: Find me a flight from SFO to JFK on United for $289.

fixtures._comment: a2ui-fixed-schema pill — one-sentence confirmation after the render (gated on toolCallId).

match.userMessage: Find me a flight from SFO to JFK on United for $289.

response.content: Here's your flight: United Airlines, SFO → JFK, $289 — rendered in the card above. Tap "Book flight" to confirm.
